title: WHAT THIS CHART
TYPE IS.
segments.body_html:
A Brainstorm or Mind Map is a radial network diagram that externalises associative thinking. A central concept occupies

What this chart type is — When rendered as a force-directed graph , the layout is computed by a physics simulation: nodes repel each other like ch

beats.text: A Brainstorm or Mind Map is a radial network diagram that externalises associative thinking. A central concept occupies the hub; related ideas radiate outward as branches; each branch can extend further into sub-concepts, creating a tree-like hierarchy that sprawls in two dimensions rather than descending top-to-bottom. When rendered as a force-directed graph , the layout is computed by a physics simulation: nodes repel each other like charged particles while edges pull connected nodes together like springs.

segments.label: 02 / 10 · WHAT THIS CHART TYPE IS

title: HOW TO READ
THIS CHART.
segments.body_html: central node→primary branch→supporting concepts→Drag any node

beats.text: Start at the central node (dark red, largest). Follow any edge outward to reach a primary branch (brass/gold). Each primary branch connects to two supporting concepts (grey-green, smallest). The spatial positions are physics-determined , proximity does not necessarily mean conceptual closeness; only edges encode actual relationships. Drag any node to reposition it manually; the simulation will adjust surrounding nodes in response. Click restart simulation to re-randomise the starting positions and observe how the physics converges to a new (but structurally equivalent) layout.

segments.label: 03 / 10 · HOW TO READ THIS CHART

title: STRENGTHS OF
THIS FORM.
segments.body_html: Spatial freedom→Immediate overview→Natural for ideation→Cross-connections

beats.text: Spatial freedom , nodes are not constrained to rows or columns, so the diagram can grow organically in any direction. Immediate overview , a viewer can see the full scope of a concept and all its associations in a single glance, even before reading individual labels. Natural for ideation , the radial structure mirrors how associative memory works: one idea triggers adjacent ideas, which trigger further associations.

segments.label: 04 / 10 · STRENGTHS OF THIS FORM

segments.section: LIMITATIONS AND WHEN NOT TO US
title: LIMITATIONS AND
WHEN NOT TO USE IT
segments.body_html: Non-deterministic layout→Does not encode quantity→Clutters at scale

beats.text: Non-deterministic layout , the force-directed positions change on each run, so a reader cannot reliably memorise where a node will appear. This makes mind maps unsuitable for reference use cases where spatial memory matters. Does not encode quantity , this form communicates structure and association, not size, frequency, or change over time. Adding quantitative data (e.g. node size = importance score) is possible but must be done carefully to avoid misleading area comparisons.

segments.label: 05 / 10 · LIMITATIONS AND WHEN NOT TO US

title: ABOUT THIS EXAMPLE —
THE IRREDUCIBLY HUMAN FRAMEWORK
segments.body_html: Irreducibly Human→Plausibility Auditing→Problem Formulation→Tool Orchestration→Interpretive Judgment

beats.text: This mind map visualises the Irreducibly Human framework: a model of the supervisory and integrative capacities that remain distinctively human even as AI systems take over execution-layer tasks. The central node is the framework itself. Five primary branches represent the core supervisory capacities that the framework argues must remain under human judgment: Plausibility Auditing (supported by Domain Knowledge and Intuition) , the ability to evaluate whether an AI output is coherent and credible given what the domain actually requires.

title: AI WAYBACK
MACHINE.
segments.body_html: Alex Osborn→Run this:→"Alex Faickney Osborn"→Now make the prompt better.

beats.text: The ideas in this chapter didn't appear from nowhere. Alex Osborn coined the word "brainstorming" in his 1953 book Applied Imagination , proposing the four rules (defer judgment, encourage wild ideas, build on others, go for quantity) that still drive most modern ideation workshops. He was a Madison Avenue ad executive. Alex Osborn, circa 1953. AI-generated portrait based on a public domain photograph (Wikimedia Commons).
